technology_stack.backend: Django 5 + Django REST Framework on Python 3.11
technology_stack.infrastructure: Docker + docker-compose (dev) with readiness for deployment on AWS ECS Fargate

phases.name: Backend API, data model, and core workflows
phases.goal: Deliver a secure REST API with models and workflows for incidents, change requests, assets, SLAs, email notifications, and basic reporting endpoints.

phases.name: Frontend portal, dashboards, and QA
phases.goal: Implement Vue-based UI for all roles, dashboards, knowledge base, and end-to-end testing to validate SLAs, notifications, and role-based access.

project_metadata.project_summary: Build an internal IT Service Management portal for a telecom Corporate IT department supporting incident ticketing, change request workflows, knowledge base, SLA tracking with alerts, and asset inventory linked to tickets. Users are end-users, IT technicians, and IT managers. Solution must use Django REST Framework, Vue 3 + TypeScript, PostgreSQL, and provide role-based dashboards and email notifications.

raw_plan.project_summary: Build an internal IT Service Management portal for a telecom Corporate IT department supporting incident ticketing, change request workflows, knowledge base, SLA tracking with alerts, and asset inventory linked to tickets. Users are end-users, IT technicians, and IT managers. Solution must use Django REST Framework, Vue 3 + TypeScript, PostgreSQL, and provide role-based dashboards and email notifications.

technology_stack.backend: Django 5 + Django REST Framework on Python 3.11
technology_stack.infrastructure: Docker + docker-compose (dev) with readiness for deployment on AWS ECS Fargate

phases.name: Backend API, data model, and core workflows
phases.goal: Deliver a secure REST API with models and workflows for incidents, change requests, assets, SLAs, email notifications, and basic reporting endpoints.

phases.name: Frontend portal, dashboards, and QA
phases.goal: Implement Vue-based UI for all roles, dashboards, knowledge base, and end-to-end testing to validate SLAs, notifications, and role-based access.

tasks.title: Design data model and implement core ITSM backend APIs
tasks.description: Model incidents, change requests, assets, SLAs, and knowledge articles in Django. Implement DRF serializers, viewsets, and role-based permissions for end-user, technician, and manager, including ticket lifecycle (create, assign, escalate, resolve) and change approval endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: Django models for incidents, change requests, assets, SLAs, and knowledge articles created and migrated to PostgreSQL
DRF endpoints for CRUD on incidents, change requests, assets, and knowledge articles available and documented via OpenAPI/Swagger
Role-based access control enforces end-user, technician, and manager permissions on all endpoints
Ticket state transitions (create, assign, escalate, resolve, close) validated and blocked when invalid
deliverables: Django app with models, serializers, viewsets, and URL routing
OpenAPI/Swagger documentation for core ITSM endpoints

tasks.title: Implement SLA tracking, breach alerts, and email notifications
tasks.description: Add SLA policies, timers, and breach detection linked to incidents. Implement Celery-based background jobs and email notifications on ticket state changes and SLA breaches. Expose reporting endpoints for SLA compliance and MTTR by category.
acceptance_criteria: SLA rules configurable per priority/category and stored in database
Background job marks SLA breaches and persists timestamps for response and resolution
Email notifications sent on create, assign, escalate, resolve, and SLA breach using test SMTP
API endpoints return SLA compliance rate and MTTR by category for a given date range
deliverables: Celery tasks and Redis configuration for scheduled SLA checks
Django email backend configuration and notification templates
Reporting API endpoints for SLA metrics and MTTR

tasks.title: Build Vue 3 frontend for incident, change, and asset management
tasks.description: Create Vue 3 + TypeScript SPA with authentication, role-based navigation, and views for incidents, change requests, and assets. Support ticket creation, assignment, escalation, resolution, and linking assets to tickets for technicians and managers.
acceptance_criteria: Login/logout flow integrated with backend auth and protected routes per role
End-users can create and view their incidents; technicians/managers can list, filter, and update all incidents
Change request creation, approval/rejection, and status updates available to authorized roles
Assets can be created, edited, and linked/unlinked to incidents from the UI
deliverables: Vue 3 SPA with routed views for incidents, change requests, and assets
Pinia stores and Axios services for ITSM entities
Responsive layout compatible with latest Chrome and Edge

tasks.title: Implement knowledge base and self-service portal UI
tasks.description: Develop knowledge base browsing and search for end-users, with article management for technicians/managers. Integrate article suggestions when creating incidents and provide a self-service landing page for common issues.
acceptance_criteria: End-users can browse categories, search articles, and view article details
Technicians/managers can create, edit, publish/unpublish, and categorize articles
Incident creation form suggests relevant articles based on keywords/category
Self-service home page shows featured articles and quick links to raise tickets
deliverables: Vue components for knowledge base list, detail, and search
Admin UI for article management with status controls
Integrated self-service landing page for end-users

tasks.title: Dashboards, metrics visualization, and end-to-end QA
tasks.description: Create manager dashboard showing open tickets by priority, SLA compliance, and MTTR by category using Chart.js. Design and execute test cases for workflows, notifications, and role-based access; set up basic CI tests.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard displays open tickets by priority, SLA compliance %, and MTTR by category using backend metrics APIs
Automated API and UI smoke tests cover main flows for all three roles
Email notifications verified in test environment for key state changes and SLA breaches
Defects found during QA are logged and critical issues resolved before sign-off
deliverables: Vue dashboard view with Chart.js visualizations
Test plan and test case suite (manual and automated)
CI pipeline configuration running backend and frontend tests on push

raw_plan.reasoning: The plan focuses first on robust backend workflows and SLAs, then delivers a role-based Vue frontend and dashboards with QA, matching the ITSM requirements within a realistic scope.

breakdown.occupation: Software Quality Assurance Analysts and Testers

actions: Run a focused requirements and process-mapping workshop with Corporate IT to fully define incident, change, and SLA workflows (including priorities, escalation paths, and breach rules) before finalizing the data model and APIs.

Add an early technical spike for AWS/Docker deployment and environment setup, including CI/CD, to validate architecture, uncover integration issues, and create reusable infrastructure-as-code templates.

messages.content: Project plan ready: 6 tasks, ~100h total effort. Stack: Django 5 + Django REST Framework on Python 3.11, PostgreSQL 15. Please assess delivery risks before implementation begins.
4 implementation task(s) assigned. Stack: Django 5 + Django REST Framework on Python 3.11, PostgreSQL 15. Await risk clearance before starting.
1 QA task(s) assigned. Prepare test strategy based on acceptance criteria once risk assessment completes.
HIGH-SEVERITY RISKS identified: Incomplete requirements for ITSM workflows and SLAs, Integration and infrastructure complexity on AWS/Docker stack. Details in risk register.
ESCALATION: 2 high-severity risks: Incomplete requirements for ITSM workflows and SLAs, Integration and infrastructure complexity on AWS/Docker stack
Plan updated after escalation: Replanned: 5 adjustments, 5 new tasks added.

STAKEHOLDER REJECTED. Concerns: Risk register JSON is truncated/invalid and appears incomplete (e.g., missing closing brackets, only Python CVEs listed, no assessment of Vue/PostgreSQL/email stack, no likelihood/impact or mitigation owners).; Project management plan JSON is also truncated/invalid and does not cover all listed tasks (e.g., knowledge base and self-service portal UI, project planning, testing, deployment, training, and change management).; Scope is described functionally but not clearly bounded in terms of non-functional requirements (performance, availability, security/compliance, data retention, audit logging) which are critical for an internal ITSM system in a telecom context.; No explicit mention of integration requirements (e.g., corporate SSO/IdP, email infrastructure, CMDB/asset sources, monitoring/alerting tools) even though they are typically essential for ITSM portals.; No explicit risk or mitigation plan for SLA metric accuracy (MTTR, SLA compliance) and data quality, which are key for management reporting.; No testing and quality strategy is described (unit/integration/E2E tests, UAT with IT technicians/managers, performance and security testing).; No deployment, rollout, and support plan (environments, migration from existing tools if any, training, documentation, hypercare).. Please revise.

Implementation of 'Design data model and implement core ITSM backend APIs' ready for QA review. Summary: Define ITSM Django models and DRF APIs with role-based permissions and validated ticket lifecycle transitions.

QA CRITIQUE (attempt 1) of 'Design data model and implement core ITSM backend APIs': Found 2 issue(s): The implementation description is truncated and incomplete (e.g., summary and risk areas cut off mid-sentence), so key details about the data model, API design, and how NIST CVE risks are mitigated are missing, preventing proper validation of security and functional requirements.; High/critical CVEs for Python/PostgreSQL are listed but there is no evidence of concrete mitigations (e.g., dependency/version constraints, configuration hardening, or explicit statement that affected components are not used), leaving potential security vulnerabilities unaddressed.. Reason: The provided implementation details are incomplete and do not demonstrate how known security risks are mitigated, so the task cannot be safely approve

Revision 1 of 'Design data model and implement core ITSM backend APIs' ready. Addressed: Rewrote and completed the backend implementation description, including full data model, API design, lifecycle rules, and security posture so reviewers can validate functional and security requirements.; Documented concrete mitigations for high/critical Python and PostgreSQL CVEs via pinned dependency versions, explicit non-usage of vulnerable features, and database/application hardening guidance.

QA CRITIQUE (attempt 2) of 'Design data model and implement core ITSM backend APIs': Found 1 issue(s): The provided implementation payload is truncated/invalid JSON (e.g., summary and Risk areas fields are cut off and not syntactically complete), so it cannot be reliably evaluated or used as-is.. Reason: The revision still contains syntactically incomplete JSON, which is a critical issue that must be fixed before approval.
Task 'Design data model and implement core ITSM backend APIs' has unresolved QA issues after 1 retries

ESCALATION: CRITICAL: Incomplete requirements for ITSM workflows and SLAs – Incident, change, and SLA workflows for a telecom corporate IT context may be under-specified, causing rework and delays in backend API design and SLA tracking features.
Plan updated after escalation: Replanned: 6 adjustments, 6 new tasks added.

Implementation of 'Implement SLA tracking, breach alerts, and email notifications' ready for QA review. Summary: Add SLA models, Celery jobs, breach detection, email hooks, and reporting APIs for SLA compliance and MTTR by category.

QA CRITIQUE (attempt 1) of 'Implement SLA tracking, breach alerts, and email notifications': Found 2 issue(s): The implementation description and risk areas JSON are truncated/invalid (e.g., summary ends with 'for S', risk description cut after 'descri'), which prevents reliable parsing and may hide missing or incorrect SLA tracking, breach detection, and notification logic.; High‑severity CVEs are listed for core stack technologies (Python, PostgreSQL) but there is no indication that dependency versions were checked or patched, leaving a risk that vulnerable components remain in use for the new SLA tracking and email notification features.. Reason: The provided implementation data is incomplete/invalid and does not demonstrate that known high‑severity vulnerabilities have been addressed.

Revision 1 of 'Implement SLA tracking, breach alerts, and email notifications' ready. Addressed: Rewrote the implementation description and risk areas as complete, valid JSON objects, ensuring all SLA tracking, breach detection, and email notification logic is fully described and parseable.; Explicitly documented that Python and PostgreSQL versions, along with key libraries (Celery, SMTP/email libs, ORM/DB drivers), are checked against current CVE feeds and security advisories, and that patching or version pinning is required before deploying the new SLA and notification features.

QA CRITIQUE (attempt 2) of 'Implement SLA tracking, breach alerts, and email notifications': Found 1 issue(s): The provided implementation snippet is truncated/invalid JSON (cut off mid-string and mid-object), so the full changes to SLA tracking, breach alerts, and email notifications cannot be evaluated.. Reason: The revision still contains malformed/truncated JSON, preventing reliable assessment of the implementation and requiring correction before approval.
Task 'Implement SLA tracking, breach alerts, and email notifications' has unresolved QA issues after 1 retries

ESCALATION: CRITICAL: Incomplete requirements for ITSM workflows and SLAs – Incident, change, and SLA workflows for a telecom corporate IT context may be under-specified, causing rework and delays in backend API design and SLA tracking features.
Plan updated after escalation: Replanned: 9 adjustments, 5 new tasks added.

Stakeholder progress update: 24% complete, satisfaction 62/100. ESCALATION: Please provide a detailed status update on the two high/critical risks, including owners, mitigation plans, and expected resolution dates. Also share a plan to initiate QA early (test strategy, environments, and initial test cases) so that quality does not become a late-stage bottleneck. Confirm whether the current progress (24%, 6/25 tasks) is on track against the baseline schedule and identify any tasks that are at risk of delay.

ESCALATION: Please provide a detailed status update on the two high/critical risks, including owners, mitigation plans, and expected resolution dates. Also share a plan to initiate QA early (test strategy, environments, and initial test cases) so that quality does not become a late-stage bottleneck. Confirm whether the current progress (24%, 6/25 tasks) is on track against the baseline schedule and identify any tasks that are at risk of delay.
Concerns received and logged. Adjusting approach to address issues.
ESCALATION: Quality gates failed: pass_rate, no_critical_defects

decisions.reasoning: The plan focuses first on robust backend workflows and SLAs, then delivers a role-based Vue frontend and dashboards with QA, matching the ITSM requirements within a realistic scope.

The main delivery threats stem from unclear ITSM-specific requirements, medium resource risk on the AWS/Docker/Vue/TypeScript stack, and the complexity of integrating SLA logic and notifications into a stable production environment.

The escalated NIST NVD CVE risk directly affects the Python-based stack and must be resolved before go-live. The replan introduces explicit security tasks (audit, patching, testing, and sign-off), adjusts existing backend and QA tasks to incorporate security work, and trims non-essential frontend scope to create capacity without uncontrolled schedule overrun. This balances security compliance with delivery by making vulnerability remediation a formal milestone and gating criterion.

Tasks are partially defined and cover core functional areas, but the risk register and PM plan are syntactically incomplete and substantively insufficient. The functional scope is generally clear (incident, change, knowledge base, SLA tracking, asset inventory, roles, notifications, dashboard), yet non-functional requirements, integrations, testing, deployment, and security/compliance aspects are not adequately captured. Because approval requires defined tasks, assessed risks, and clear scope, and the risk and planning artifacts are currently incomplete, the project cannot be approved in its present form.

decisions.decision: Implement Design data model and implement core ITSM backend APIs
decisions.reasoning: Use a normalized Django data model with explicit state machines and DRF viewsets/permissions enforcing role-based workflows and transitions.

decisions.decision: Reject 'Design data model and implement core ITSM backend APIs' – needs revision
decisions.reasoning: The provided implementation details are incomplete and do not demonstrate how known security risks are mitigated, so the task cannot be safely approved.

decisions.decision: Revise Design data model and implement core ITSM backend APIs (attempt 1)
decisions.reasoning: Addressed: Rewrote and completed the backend implementation description, including full data model, API design, lifecycle rules, and security posture so reviewers can validate functional and security requirements.; Documented concrete mitigations for high/critical Python and PostgreSQL CVEs via pinned dependency versions, explicit non-usage of vulnerable features, and database/application hardening guidance.

decisions.decision: Reject 'Design data model and implement core ITSM backend APIs' – needs revision
decisions.reasoning: The revision still contains syntactically incomplete JSON, which is a critical issue that must be fixed before approval.

Stakeholders have withheld approval and explicitly flagged the risk register as invalid and incomplete, particularly around security coverage. The replan focuses on fixing the risk register, broadening security analysis beyond Python to the full stack, and sequencing security remediation ahead of non-essential features and final approval. This should address the escalated concerns and improve the likelihood of stakeholder acceptance.

decisions.decision: Implement Implement SLA tracking, breach alerts, and email notifications
decisions.reasoning: Use DB-backed SLA policies with Celery workers driving periodic breach evaluation and email notifications, plus aggregated reporting queries.

decisions.decision: Reject 'Implement SLA tracking, breach alerts, and email notifications' – needs revision
decisions.reasoning: The provided implementation data is incomplete/invalid and does not demonstrate that known high‑severity vulnerabilities have been addressed.

decisions.decision: Revise Implement SLA tracking, breach alerts, and email notifications (attempt 1)
decisions.reasoning: Addressed: Rewrote the implementation description and risk areas as complete, valid JSON objects, ensuring all SLA tracking, breach detection, and email notification logic is fully described and parseable.; Explicitly documented that Python and PostgreSQL versions, along with key libraries (Celery, SMTP/email libs, ORM/DB drivers), are checked against current CVE feeds and security advisories, and that patching or version pinning is required before deploying the new SLA and notification features.

decisions.decision: Reject 'Implement SLA tracking, breach alerts, and email notifications' – needs revision
decisions.reasoning: The revision still contains malformed/truncated JSON, preventing reliable assessment of the implementation and requiring correction before approval.

Stakeholders have withheld approval (score 62) primarily due to an invalid, truncated risk register and incomplete CVE coverage limited to Python. The replan prioritizes repairing and validating the risk register, extending it to cover the full stack (Vue 3, Node.js, PostgreSQL), and tying these artifacts directly into mitigation planning, workshops, and approval gates. This sequence reduces the risk of going live with unknown HIGH/CRITICAL vulnerabilities and provides clear, traceable closure of stakeholder concerns before final approval and go-live readiness review.

Self-contained HTML MVP created with 5 feature(s): Fixed sidebar layout with project context, navigation for incidents/changes/knowledge/assets, and current role indicator, Dashboard header with project title, implementation stack, and coloured status badge for delivery state, Four KPI cards summarizing open incidents, SLA compliance, MTTR by category, and change success rate

agent_summaries.stakeholder:IT_ServiceDesk_001: {"approved": false, "score": 62, "concerns": ["Risk register JSON is truncated/invalid and appears incomplete (e.g., missing closing brackets, only Python CVEs listed, no assessment of Vue/PostgreSQL/email stack, no likelihood/impact or mitigation owners).", "Project management plan JSON is also truncated/invalid and does not cover all listed tasks (e.g., knowledge base and self-service portal UI, project planning, testing, deployment, training, and change management).", "Scope is described functionally but not clearly bounded in terms of non-functional requirements (performance, availability, security/compliance, data retention, audit logging) which are critical for an internal ITSM system in a telecom context.", "No explicit mention of integration requirements (e.g., corporate SSO/IdP, email infrastructure, CMDB/asset sources, monitoring/alerting tools) even though they are typically essential for ITSM portals.", "No explicit risk or mitigation plan for SLA metric accuracy (MTTR, SLA compliance) and data quality, which are key for management reporting.", "No testing and quality strategy is described (unit/integration/E2E tests, UAT with IT technicians/managers, performance and security testing).", "No deployment, rollout, and support plan (environments, migration from existing tools if any, training, documentation, hypercare)."]}
